title: Step 101: Spawn-only ablation -- forgetting is class interference

content.purpose: Determine whether CIFAR-100 forgetting comes from codebook update drift or class-incremental interference.
content.method: Run identical CIFAR-100 protocol with lr=0 (no updates, spawn only).
content.result: DISPROVED update drift hypothesis. lr=0 identical to lr=0.001. Forgetting is class-incremental interference, not codebook corruption.
content.key_insight: The codebook is stable. Forgetting is structural: more classes means more competition for top-k slots.

constraints_implied: fc014: CIFAR-100 forgetting is class competition, not codebook corruption
